target.title: fix(ui): avoid duplicate pre-tool text in chat stream after tool boundary
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: When agent streaming produces text → tool → more text within one run, the post-tool segment displayed below the tool card includes the pre-tool text again. Pre-tool text is committed into `chatStreamSegments` (rendered above the tool card) on tool start, but the next chat delta arrives as a full-snapshot text from the gateway and gets assigned to `chatStream` as-is, so [`buildChatItems`](ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts) renders the pre-tool prefix twice.
- **Why it matters**: Visible duplicate assistant text after every tool invocation degrades readability and trust in the streaming output for Control UI users.
- **What changed (UI only)**: Track the cumulative upstream snapshot length committed to `chatStreamSegments` in a new `chatStreamCommittedLen` state field. [`handleAgentEvent`](ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts) bumps it on each tool-start commit, [`handleChatEvent`](ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts) slices the post-tool delta off that prefix before assigning to `chatStream`, and all run-end paths reset it.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: Gateway chat-delta payloads remain monotonic full snapshots (no [`server-chat.ts`](src/gateway/server-chat.ts) / [`chat-display-projection.ts`](src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts) / protocol changes). ACP ([`handleDeltaEvent`](src/acp/translator.ts) `fullText.length` vs `sentTextLength` math), TUI (`TuiStreamAssembler.ingestDelta`), and any other non-segment-aware WS subscriber are untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #54374 (supersedes the prior gateway-side attempt; pivoted to a UI-only fix per `clawsweeper` review feedback after the gateway path raised ACP/TUI compatibility concerns)
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- **Root cause**: Gateway broadcasts chat deltas as monotonic full-snapshot text (pre-tool + post-tool concatenated). UI commits the in-progress `chatStream` into `chatStreamSegments` at tool start (so it renders above the tool card) and clears `chatStream`, but the next delta arrives as the full snapshot again and `handleChatEvent` reassigns the pre-tool prefix to `chatStream`, so [`buildChatItems`](ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts) renders `streamSegments[i]` (pre-tool) and the active `stream` (pre-tool + post-tool) back to back.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: The UI had no record of how many characters of the upstream snapshot had already been committed to segments, so it could not derive a post-tool-only slice from the next full snapshot.
- **Prior context**: PR #54374 originally attempted to solve this by tracking `segmentOffsets` on the gateway side and emitting segment-relative delta text. `clawsweeper` flagged that segment-relative payloads would regress ACP and TUI consumers that rely on monotonic full snapshots, and recommended keeping gateway deltas as full snapshots while doing the post-tool projection inside the Control UI. After maintainer commit `5f2273e81e fix(gateway): unify chat display projection` introduced [`chat-display-projection.ts`](src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts) and [`live-chat-projector.ts`](src/gateway/live-chat-projector.ts) (which only handle suppress concerns, not tool-boundary segmentation), this PR pivots to the recommended UI-only approach.
- **Why this regressed now**: Latent. Visible whenever a model produces assistant text both before and after a tool call within the same run.
- **If unknown, what was ruled out**: N/A.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: [`ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`](ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts), [`ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts`](ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts)
- Scenario the test should lock in: When `handleAgentEvent` commits a stream segment on tool start, `chatStreamCommittedLen` must be bumped by the segment text length; when `handleChatEvent` receives a subsequent full-snapshot delta, it must slice off the committed prefix before assigning to `chatStream`; all run-end paths (`final` / `aborted` / `error` / `sendChatMessage` start / `resetToolStream` / session switch) must reset the counter to 0.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Pure unit tests on `handleChatEvent` and `handleAgentEvent` with synthetic payloads can fully exercise the text → tool → text path without requiring a live gateway, ACP runtime, or rendered DOM.
- Existing test that already covers this: None before this PR.
- New tests added: 7 in [`ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`](ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts) (slice prefix, accumulate across tools, fallback to full text when stale, no-op when no committed prefix, terminal-state reset for `final` / `aborted` / `error`); 4 in [`ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts`](ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts) (single boundary commits and bumps, multiple boundaries accumulate, no-op on null stream, no-op on whitespace-only stream).

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Control UI: streaming chat text shown after a tool card no longer repeats the pre-tool text that was already rendered above the tool card. The committed segment, the tool card, and the active streaming bubble line up cleanly.
- Final / aborted / error chat messages and the persisted transcript are unaffected (we only change which prefix of the in-flight stream is rendered live).
- TUI, ACP, and any other non-Control-UI subscriber: no change.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.
- Data access scope changed? No.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+ / Bun
- Model/provider: Any model that uses tool calls (e.g. Claude with tools)
- Integration/channel: Web chat / Control UI
- Relevant config (redacted): Default gateway config

### Steps

1. Open the Control UI Web chat with an agent that has tools enabled.
2. Send a prompt that triggers the model to produce text, invoke a tool, then produce more text (e.g. "Execute 3 file read tools, sending a short message before each execution.").
3. Observe the streaming text rendered after the tool card while the second segment streams in.

### Expected

- Only the post-tool text streams in below the tool card; the pre-tool text remains exactly once, in the committed segment above the tool card.

### Actual

- (Before fix) The pre-tool text reappears below the tool card and visibly duplicates while the post-tool text streams in.
- (After fix) Only post-tool text streams in the new segment.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```
$ pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-ui.config.ts \
    ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts \
    ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts
 ✓ unit-ui  ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts (57 tests) 19ms
 ✓ unit-ui  ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts (4 tests) 2ms
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  61 passed (61)

$ pnpm test ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts
 ✓ ui  ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts (11 tests) 6ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)

$ pnpm test src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts
 ✓ gateway  src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts (52 tests) 51ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  52 passed (52)
```

(Screenshots from the previous attempt at PR #54374 still illustrate the visible bug; this fix produces the same post-fix rendering with zero gateway-side changes.)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Triggered text → tool → text streaming via the Control UI Web chat with multiple sequential tool calls in one run; confirmed delta payloads remain full snapshots on the wire and the rendered chat shows the pre-tool text exactly once.
- Edge cases checked: run with no tool calls (`chatStreamCommittedLen` stays 0, no slicing); run aborted mid-tool (`final` / `aborted` / `error` reset the counter); multiple sequential tool calls in one run (counter accumulates per boundary); session switch (counter is reset alongside `chatStreamSegments`); reconnect mid-run (`resetToolStream` clears it).
- What I did **not** verify on real hardware: TUI live wiring (relied on the unchanged gateway delta contract and the existing 52 gateway tests); ACP live session (same — `handleDeltaEvent`'s `fullText.length` vs `sentTextLength` math depends only on monotonic full snapshots, which this PR preserves).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert this single commit. `chatStreamCommittedLen` is a new additive UI state field with no persistence; removing it restores the previous full-snapshot assignment to `chatStream` (and the original visible-duplication bug).
- Files/config to restore: [`ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts`](ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts), [`ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts`](ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts), [`ui/src/ui/app.ts`](ui/src/ui/app.ts), [`ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts`](ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts), [`ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.ts`](ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.ts) (plus the two test files).
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: blank chat bubble below the tool card (would mean the slice offset is too large); pre-tool text still duplicating (would mean the bump was missed at a tool boundary); leftover stale offset on a new run (would mean a reset path was missed).

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: A run mutates `chatStream` / `chatStreamSegments` outside the documented paths (e.g. an external controller resets `chatStream` without resetting `chatStreamCommittedLen`), leaving a stale offset that would trim the next delta incorrectly.
  - **Mitigation**: All known reset points are aligned: `resetToolStream` ([`app-tool-stream.ts`](ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts)) clears the counter alongside `chatStreamSegments`, `handleChatEvent` clears it on every owned `final` / `aborted` / `error`, `sendChatMessage` clears it when starting a new run, `resetChatStateForSessionSwitch` ([`app-render.helpers.ts`](ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.ts)) clears it on session switch, and the reconnect path in [`app-gateway.ts`](ui/src/ui/app-gateway.ts) calls `resetToolStream` indirectly. `handleChatEvent`'s defensive fallback (`next.length <= committedLen → keep raw text`) prevents a stale offset from producing an empty stream.
- **Risk**: A future refactor of [`chat-display-projection.ts`](src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts) or [`live-chat-projector.ts`](src/gateway/live-chat-projector.ts) starts emitting non-monotonic deltas (e.g. trimming pre-tool text on the wire), which would invalidate the slice math.
  - **Mitigation**: The 52 gateway agent-event tests in [`src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts`](src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts) already lock in monotonic full-snapshot deltas for the bridge path, and this PR adds an explicit fallback (`next.length <= committedLen → keep raw text`) so any future non-monotonic delta degrades gracefully rather than silently dropping characters.

target.title: WhatsApp: send commentary live
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: WhatsApp suppressed assistant commentary-phase text entirely, so long-running agent tasks could look silent until the final answer.
- Why it matters: operators asked for opt-in live progress updates on WhatsApp without changing default delivery/privacy behavior.
- What changed: added opt-in `channels.whatsapp.commentaryDelivery = "live"` / account-level overrides, live text-only commentary delivery, final-reply dedupe for delivered commentary, abort/timeout bounds, docs, changelog, and Plugin SDK runtime documentation.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): commentary remains off by default, WhatsApp-only, text-only, and still suppressed for `message_tool_only` source-delivery routes.

AI-assisted: yes. I used Codex for implementation, local review, and validation, and addressed the local review findings before opening this PR.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #57484
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: current WhatsApp delivery treated assistant `commentary` phase as suppressed visible output with no opt-in channel delivery path.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no WhatsApp test covered live commentary extraction, text-only directive stripping, final-answer dedupe, silent-turn suppression, or source-delivery policy suppression.
- Contributing context (if known): closed PR #57484 had the original feature shape, but current `main` moved WhatsApp delivery ownership into `inbound-dispatch.ts` and changed runner fallback behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/pi-embedded-commentary.test.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.commentary.test.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/deliver-reply.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: live commentary is opt-in, text-only, silent-policy aware, source-delivery-policy aware, bounded by abort/timeouts, and not duplicated in the final reply.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: helper tests lock text extraction/dedupe; subscriber and WhatsApp dispatch tests lock the integration boundaries without requiring a live WhatsApp session.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing commentary suppression and block/final reply tests covered no-callback behavior but not live delivery.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Adds an opt-in WhatsApp config:

```json5
{
  channels: {
    whatsapp: {
      commentaryDelivery: "live",
    },
  },
}
```

The default remains `"off"`. Account-level `channels.whatsapp.accounts.<id>.commentaryDelivery` overrides the channel default.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
assistant commentary -> suppressed -> final reply only

After:
assistant commentary -> commentaryDelivery off -> suppressed
assistant commentary -> commentaryDelivery live -> text-only WhatsApp status message -> final reply dedupes delivered commentary
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: opt-in live commentary can send intermediate assistant status text over WhatsApp. It remains default-off, respects `silentExpected` and `message_tool_only`, strips/suppresses media/audio/reply directives, suppresses reasoning/tool/compaction payloads, and is bounded by abort/timeout handling.

## Repro + Verification

- Model/provider: OpenAI Codex during live local testing

### Steps

1. Enable WhatsApp `commentaryDelivery: "live"`.
2. Run a WhatsApp prompt that asks the agent to send commentary while doing work.
3. Observe commentary during the task, then final answer without duplicate commentary.

### Expected

- Live text-only commentary is delivered only when opt-in is enabled.
- Disabled/silent/message-tool-only routes do not deliver live commentary.

### Actual

## Evidence

target.title: fix(ui): stabilize WebChat message ordering
target.body:
## Summary
- Rebased PR #74733 onto current `origin/main` and replaced the old XL patch with a focused WebChat reliability fix.
- Keep optimistic user messages visible when a stale in-flight `chat.history` response resolves after a local send.
- Avoid clearing active assistant stream/tool state while a run is still in progress.
- Clear tool-stream artifacts during `/clear`/`sessions.reset` so old stream/tool cards cannot survive a reset.
- Add a small temp-path guard cleanup for the current-main context treemap output path so `pnpm check` passes.

## Problem
WebChat can start a `chat.history` request, then the user sends a message before that request resolves. The old response was reconciled against the request-start message list, so it could overwrite the newer optimistic user message and clear the active stream. Visually, the sent message could disappear and then come back only after a later response/history refresh.

## Approach
- Track local chat mutations separately from history request versions.
- When a history response resolves after local state changed, merge against the latest visible chat state and preserve unanchored optimistic messages that are not present in the stale history payload.
- Only clear streaming/tool state from history reloads when there is no active stream/run.
- Reset tool-stream collections in the synchronous `/clear` reset path before reloading history.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** WebChat sent messages could disappear while an older `chat.history` request resolved after the local send; this patch keeps the optimistic send and active stream state visible.
- **Real environment tested:** Vladyslav's Mac mini running the real OpenClaw launchd gateway (`ai.openclaw.gateway`) on port 18789, installed from this fork via the global `/opt/homebrew/bin/openclaw` npm symlink.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Built and installed the final fork head, then restarted the real gateway: `pnpm build`, `pnpm ui:build`, `npm install -g /Users/vladyslavlevchuk/Projects/openclaw-pr-74733`, `launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/ai.openclaw.gateway`, `openclaw --version`, `curl -I http://127.0.0.1:18789/`, and log inspection of `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the real setup:

```text
$ openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.5.10-beta.1 (48efa0e)

$ launchctl print gui/501/ai.openclaw.gateway
state = running
pid = 82744
last exit code = 0
arguments = /opt/homebrew/bin/openclaw gateway --port 18789

$ tail ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log
2026-05-10T14:18:23.620-04:00 [gateway] http server listening (10 plugins: acpx, active-memory, bonjour, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 2.5s)
2026-05-10T14:18:24.289-04:00 [gateway] ready

$ curl -I http://127.0.0.1:18789/
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
Cache-Control: no-cache
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The fork is running in a real OpenClaw gateway setup and serving the rebuilt Control UI from the final PR head. The WebChat race is covered by a regression test that reproduces the stale-history response landing after a local send and asserts the optimistic user message remains visible while the active stream remains intact.
- **What was not tested:** I did not manually send a production WebChat message through the operator's live session after installing; the live setup is installed for operator verification, and stale 2026.5.7 native clients may need an app restart or browser hard refresh to reconnect to the beta gateway.

## Verification
- `pnpm build` passed.
- `pnpm ui:build` passed.
- `pnpm check` passed.
- `pnpm --dir ui exec vitest run src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts src/ui/chat/history-merge.test.ts src/ui/app-chat.test.ts --config vitest.config.ts` passed: 101 tests.
- `pnpm format:check CHANGELOG.md src/auto-reply/reply/context-treemap.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/history-merge.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts` passed.
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/context-treemap.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/history-merge.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts` passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui` passed.
- Final head `48efa0e303`: `pnpm check:changed` passed.
- Final head `48efa0e303`: `env -u OPENCLAW_ALLOW_INSECURE_PRIVATE_WS -u OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HOST -u OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN -u OPENCLAW_VAPID_SUBJECT pnpm test:changed:max` passed: 21 shards.

## Full Test Note
I also ran `pnpm test` after `pnpm build`. The branch-relevant shards passed, but the full local run failed in unrelated areas because this machine exports live OpenClaw gateway/VAPID env vars and because some broad current-main tests are sensitive to built `dist/` or unrelated mock drift. I did not fold those unrelated repairs into this WebChat PR.

## AI Assistance
AI-assisted. I reviewed the resulting diff and understand the behavior it changes.

target.title: docs(gateway): add external auth-proxy recipe
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a recipe page documenting how to use existing OpenClaw primitives (custom `baseUrl`, eager SecretRef snapshot) to build an external auth-injection proxy that closes the agent-self-read gap acknowledged in #11829 and #10659.

The page does **not** propose any core change. It documents a pattern users can apply today.

## Why now

`@steipete`'s 2026-04-26 review on #10659 explicitly framed current SecretRef + redaction + plugin hooks as **partial** hardening, and noted that native masked-secret / credential-provider boundaries are unbuilt. PR #9271 attempted a full in-core proxy and was closed unmerged at 15.9K LOC. PRs #12839 and #22744 followed similar fates.

That leaves users hitting this issue with no documented workaround. This recipe gives them one — focused on the surface OpenClaw already exposes — without staking a position on what the eventual native solution should look like.

## Field validation

Implemented and running on a 21-tenant Badclaw deployment (Hetzner CPX41) for the Anthropic provider. End-to-end verification:

- Agent container has no `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in env, in `/run/secrets/`, or anywhere on its filesystem.
- A live chat completion through the proxy returns 200 in 1326ms (Anthropic-side latency dominates; localhost-hop overhead measured at <1ms).
- Proxy access logs show source-IP-based tenant identification working as designed.

Reference implementation linked from the page: https://github.com/coletebou/Badland/tree/master/badclaw/services/auth-proxy (~150 LOC stdlib-only Node, MIT-licensed).

## What this PR doesn't do

- Doesn't add a built-in proxy. That's a much larger conversation (#9271 territory) and would need maintainer alignment on shape.
- Doesn't change SecretRef semantics, OAuth refresh, or any runtime behavior.
- Doesn't conflict with the active plugin-SDK work in #59165 / #72548 — those operate on a different bridge (credential resolution, not request rewriting).

## Test plan

- [x] Mintlify navigation entry added under "Authentication and secrets"
- [x] Verified `docs.json` parses (no orphan groups, page reachable from sidebar)
- [x] Internal links to `/gateway/secrets`, `/gateway/authentication` resolve
- [x] External links to issues/PRs (#11829, #10659, #9271) verified live

## Related

- #11829 — Security Roadmap: Protecting API Keys from Agent Access
- #10659 — Masked Secrets
- #9271 — Earlier full-proxy attempt (referenced in the page as a useful but more ambitious comparison)
- The comment I just left on #11829 with field experience: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11829#issuecomment-4342619242

target.title: fix(media): include configured roots in path-not-allowed error hint
target.body:
## Problem

`assertLocalMediaAllowed` rejects out-of-tree paths with:

```
Local media path is not under an allowed directory: /tmp/file.png
```

This tells the operator their path was wrong but not what *was* allowed. In production the user just sees the rejection; they have no way to tell whether `/tmp` was excluded, what `/root/.openclaw/media/inbound` looks like, or why moving the file would fix it. The result is "image tool failed → I have no idea where to put the file".

This shows up in real workflows where users (or other tools) drop files in `/tmp` thinking it's a generally accessible scratch dir, then hit the rejection without any hint that the runtime expects paths under the configured local roots.

## Fix

Append a short `(allowed roots: …)` hint to the `path-not-allowed` error message, listing the first 4 configured roots and a `+N more` overflow marker for the rest. The format is:

```
Local media path is not under an allowed directory: /tmp/file.png \
  (allowed roots: /root/.openclaw/workspace, /root/.openclaw/media/inbound, /root/.openclaw/media/outbound)
```

…and for misconfigured configs with many roots:

```
Local media path is not under an allowed directory: /var/elsewhere/file.png \
  (allowed roots: /tmp/openclaw-1, /tmp/openclaw-2, /tmp/openclaw-3, /tmp/openclaw-4, +3 more)
```

The 4-root cap (`ALLOWED_ROOTS_HINT_LIMIT`) keeps the error message bounded even when a config drifts large; an overflow marker with the elided count keeps the operator aware that the list is non-trivial. The message still surfaces the rejected path (unchanged contract).

Both throw sites share the new `formatAllowedRootsHint` helper so the workspace-fast-path branch and the general-loop branch produce identical error shapes.

## Tests

Two new regression cases in `src/media/local-media-access.test.ts`:

- A small allowed-roots list shows up verbatim in the rejection error and the rejected path is preserved.
- A long allowed-roots list (> 4 entries) is capped, the first roots inline, the trailing roots collapsed into a `+N more` marker, and the cap-ed entries do **not** appear verbatim in the message.

Existing tests (managed inbound media bypass, nested inbound rejection) still pass — the hint is purely additive on the existing `path-not-allowed` error.

`pnpm exec vitest run src/media/local-media-access.test.ts` is green locally (4/4).

## Notes

- `LocalMediaAccessError.code` is unchanged (`"path-not-allowed"`), so any caller filtering on the error code keeps working. Only the human-readable message body grows.
- Showing the configured roots is acceptable here because the error is server-side: it goes back to the operator (who has filesystem access anyway) and the runtime already publishes the same paths in `openclaw status`, config docs, and similar diagnostics. No new leakage.
- The hint stays inside the same single-line message so logs that grep for "Local media path is not under an allowed directory" continue to match.

target.title: fix(skills): never wipe target skills dir on sync to prevent data los…
target.body:
…s on partial load

`syncSkillsToWorkspace` used to call `fsp.rm(targetSkillsDir)` before recopying every skill. Any transient failure inside `loadWorkspaceSkillEntries` (a filesystem hiccup, a config parse error, an agent-filter edge case) returns fewer entries than expected and the wipe step destroys skills that the recopy step cannot restore. Users report their entire skills directory disappearing after a single bad load.

Switch to a non-destructive, per-skill incremental sync:

- Only `mkdir` the target skills dir, never wipe it wholesale.
- Build a manifest of (destination dir, source entry) pairs up front, preserving the existing duplicate-basename disambiguation.
- For each entry compare source vs destination mtime via a small 2-level `getNewestMtime` helper; skip re-copying when the destination is already at least as new.
- When a copy is needed, scope the `rm` to that single destination dir so stale files inside it cannot leak into the new copy, then `cp`.
- Emit a debug summary (`manifest/copied/skipped/failed`) to make post-sync diagnostics possible without reconstructing state.

Target directories that are not part of the source manifest — for example skills the user installed directly inside a sandbox — are left untouched. Sandboxes are ephemeral, so a few leftover directories are cheap; losing real user data is not.

Add four regression tests in the existing sync suite:

- Second sync with an empty source no longer wipes previously synced skills.
- User-created skill directories survive an unrelated source sync.
- Unchanged skills are not re-copied (destination mtime preserved).
- Modified source skills are picked up and re-copied.

target.title: fix: handle undefined message/text at runtime to prevent TypeError
target.body:
## Problem

When `message` / `payload.text` is `undefined` at runtime (e.g., across RPC boundaries), the code crashes with:
```
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')
```

TypeScript type annotations only apply at compile time, but runtime values crossing process boundaries (gateway RPC, Electron IPC) can be undefined.

## Solution

Add defensive runtime type checking before calling string methods:

1. **`sanitizeChatSendMessageInput`**: Return an error immediately if message is not a string
2. **`normalizeReplyPayload`**: Normalize the type of `payload.text` before using optional chaining

This ensures graceful error handling instead of crashing.

---

Found and fixed while debugging EasyClaw (which uses OpenClaw as a vendor submodule).

target.title: feat(webchat): deliver video uploads natively
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: #27482 needs WebChat/Control UI video uploads, and the first pass only staged clips as managed media without native model delivery.
- Why it matters: video-capable models such as Gemini should receive supported chat clips directly, while non-video-capable or fallback-selected models must still get a readable managed-media path instead of silently losing the attachment.
- What changed: WebChat now accepts/previews MP4/M4V/MOV/WebM uploads, the Gateway validates video attachment MIME/extension signals, video-capable catalog metadata flows through model selection, Google receives native video content blocks when the active model advertises `video`, and the embedded runner falls back to `MediaPaths`-style prompt notes when the final runtime model cannot consume video.
- Safety hardening: the Gateway rejects renamed non-video payloads like PDFs masquerading as `.mp4`, and OpenAI/Anthropic transports no longer reinterpret video blocks as image inputs.
- Changelog: kept the user-facing `CHANGELOG.md` entry. Repo history shows user-facing PRs commonly include changelog entries, with maintainers adding separate backfills only when entries were missed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #27482
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Design Notes

- Root cause: chat attachment handling had image-specific native delivery and generic non-image staging, but no video-aware contract spanning UI allowlists, Gateway validation, model capability metadata, and provider transport payloads.
- Guardrails added: native video delivery is gated by model catalog `input: ["video"]`; unsupported or final-runtime-incompatible media is converted back to managed media notes rather than being dropped; concrete non-video MIME sniffing wins over misleading video filenames.
- Provider boundary: Google transport supports native video blocks; OpenAI/Anthropic transports intentionally ignore video blocks instead of casting them to image blocks.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should catch this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
- Target tests/files:
  - `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/chat-attachments.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts`
  - `extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
  - `extensions/google/provider-models.test.ts`
  - catalog/session utility tests already in the PR
- Scenario locked in: supported videos are accepted/previewed/staged; unsupported video formats and MIME/extension mismatches are rejected; native video is delivered only to video-capable models; final model switches preserve fallback access; non-video transports do not miscast videos as images.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Control UI/WebChat accepts MP4, M4V, MOV, and WebM chat uploads, shows video previews, and rejects known unsupported video containers. When the selected provider/model supports video input, supported clips can be delivered natively; otherwise the agent receives managed media path context for fallback handling.

## Diagram

```text
Supported video model:
[select clip.mp4] -> [UI preview] -> [chat.send] -> [native video block]

Non-video/fallback model:
[select clip.mp4] -> [UI preview] -> [chat.send] -> [managed media path note]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No.
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No.
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: WebChat can now upload supported video formats through the existing attachment path. The change keeps existing size limits, stores clips under managed inbound media, validates the supported format allowlist in UI and Gateway, rejects misleading MIME/filename mismatches, and gates native delivery on model capability metadata.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: local Linux dev workspace
- Runtime/container: Node v24.12.0, pnpm v10.33.0
- Model/provider: test fixtures plus Google transport coverage
- Integration/channel: Control UI/WebChat + Gateway `chat.send`

### Steps

1. Select an MP4/WebM/MOV/M4V file in the Control UI chat composer.
2. Send the chat message to a model catalog entry with `input` containing `video`.
3. Repeat with a text-only/final-fallback model and with a renamed non-video payload.

### Expected

- Supported videos are accepted and previewed.
- Video-capable models receive native video blocks.
- Non-video-capable final models receive managed-media fallback notes.
- Renamed non-video payloads are rejected before native delivery.

## Evidence

Passing local checks:

- `pnpm test src/gateway/chat-attachments.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:all`
- `pnpm lint:core`
- `pnpm check:changed -- --base upstream/main`

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: supported video UI acceptance/preview tests, unsupported video rejection, Gateway native/fallback routing, final-model media filtering, Google native video transport, OpenAI/Anthropic non-misclassification, and narrowed changed gate against current `upstream/main`.
- Edge cases checked: MIME sniff mismatch with supported video filename, text-only final model fallback, video-capable model without image input, and transport replay with mixed text/media content.
- What you did **not** verify: live provider API upload behavior outside mocked transport coverage.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes.
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No.
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No.
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: provider catalogs may incorrectly advertise video support.
  - Mitigation: final runtime filtering preserves fallback notes when the actual selected model lacks video input.
- Risk: malformed or renamed files could reach provider-native media paths.
  - Mitigation: concrete sniffed non-video MIME rejects misleading supported video filenames before native delivery.
- Risk: non-video transports could mis-handle new media blocks.
  - Mitigation: OpenAI and Anthropic conversions now ignore video blocks instead of converting them to image payloads.

target.title: fix(gateway): add cron-list transport timing diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

Adds **default-off** Gateway client transport timing diagnostics to help explain latency between fast in-handler `cron.list` stages and slower end-to-end WebSocket timing (audit context: handler ~23 ms vs ~638 ms WS line in isolated runs).

## How to enable

Set exactly:

`OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_CLIENT_TIMING_DEBUG=1`

Unset or any other value → **no** timing lines.

## Emitted fields (allow-listed)

Each line is `gateway.client.timing` + JSON with only:

- `stage`, `elapsedMs`, `ok`, `method`, `requestKind`, optional `errorName` / `errorCode`

No URLs, tokens, paths, params, job bodies, prompts, or session data.

## Stages

Covers `executeGatewayRequestWithScopes_entered` → `event_loop_ready` → `ws_open` → `connect_handshake` → `hello_ok` → per-RPC `request_send` / `response_wait` / `frame_receive_parse` / `request_settle` → `command_complete`.

## Tests

- Debug gate (`1` vs unset)
- Allow-listed payload shape
- Hostile sentinel strings do not appear in log output
- Sanitizer rejects unknown stages / invalid payloads

## Windows note

If `[openclaw] shell env fallback failed` appears during local build/test, it is environmental; this PR does not change shell probing beyond Gateway timing.

target.title: feat(bedrock): Cline-parity config surface + BedrockAuthConfig helper
target.body:
## Summary

Brings the amazon-bedrock plugin's declared config surface to 1:1 parity with Cline's Bedrock UI and pi-ai's refactored Bedrock auth contract (pending upstream in https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/pull/3947).

The actual Bedrock inference changes land in pi-ai. This PR declares the config keys pi-ai consumes so OpenClaw's manifest-driven discovery and setup UX can expose them, and adds a small typed helper for normalizing legacy OpenClaw config bags into the four explicit Bedrock auth modes.

### Added

- `BedrockAuthConfig`, `BedrockAuthenticationMode`, `LegacyBedrockOptions`, and `ReasoningEffort` types in `extensions/amazon-bedrock/bedrock-auth-config.ts`. Four-way mode dispatch (`apikey` / `profile` / `credentials` / `default`) with legacy `awsUseProfile: true` migrating transparently to `awsAuthentication: "profile"`.
- `normalizeBedrockAuthConfig(legacy) -> BedrockAuthConfig` — pure helper that resolves the auth mode, normalizes defaults (`awsRegion: "us-east-1"`, `awsUseCrossRegionInference: true`, `awsUseGlobalInference: true`, `awsBedrockUsePromptCache: true`, `enable1MContext: false`), and drops invalid `reasoningEffort` values.
- `BedrockSetupOptions` type alias re-exported from `extensions/amazon-bedrock/setup-api.ts` for setup/onboarding surfaces.
- Plugin-manifest `configSchema` and `uiHints` declarations in `extensions/amazon-bedrock/openclaw.plugin.json` for the full Cline-parity surface: `awsAuthentication`, `awsBedrockApiKey`, `awsProfile`, `awsAccessKey`, `awsSecretKey`, `awsSessionToken`, `awsRegion`, `awsBedrockEndpoint`, `awsUseCrossRegionInference`, `awsUseGlobalInference`, `awsBedrockUsePromptCache`, `reasoningEffort`, `thinkingBudgetTokens`, `enable1MContext`.
- Docs: new "Explicit auth modes and Cline-parity config" accordion in `docs/providers/bedrock.md` covering the four auth modes and the full config table.
- Changelog bullets under `## Unreleased` / `### Changes` with `Thanks @praxstack` attribution.

### Tests

- `extensions/amazon-bedrock/bedrock-auth-config.test.ts` — 13 tests covering the six baseline normalize cases, coverage-gap cases (reasoningEffort enum validation, explicit boolean override survival, resolveMode precedence pair), and a runtime structural check that `BedrockSetupOptions` from `setup-api.ts` is a valid alias for `BedrockAuthConfig`.

### Not in this PR

- Actual Bedrock inference auth changes land in pi-ai. Tracking: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/pull/3947. Once that ships, a follow-up will bump the `@mariozechner/pi-ai` pin and retire OpenClaw's local patch overlay.
- A runtime `resolveAuth(cfg) -> ResolvedBedrockClientConfig` helper is intentionally left to pi-ai rather than duplicated here, so the plugin stays a config-surface owner while pi-ai remains the inference/auth resolver.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: OpenClaw users on legacy Bedrock configs (`awsUseProfile: true`, raw `awsBedrockApiKey`, raw access/secret pairs) had no normalization layer producing a stable `BedrockAuthConfig` shape for the manifest-driven Cline-parity setup surface. After this patch, `normalizeBedrockAuthConfig(legacy)` yields the documented four-mode dispatch from any historical OpenClaw config bag.

**Real environment tested**: Local macOS 26.5 (Apple Silicon) checkout of `praxstack/openclaw` at the rebased PR head `fe6014339289bc039e368c1df94fe0729fd0b39d` (6 ahead, 0 behind `openclaw/openclaw@aef93881af5`). Bun 1.3.11, Node v24.3.0. No mocks; the helper is invoked directly against representative legacy config bags collected from real OpenClaw migration scenarios.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: A throwaway TypeScript driver (`bedrock-auth-config.realbehavior-driver.ts`, untracked, not part of the diff) imports the published helper and exercises it against five realistic legacy config bags — `awsUseProfile=true` with a stale apikey ALSO set, explicit Cline-parity apikey mode, STS credentials with session token, default mode with a bogus `reasoningEffort`, and an empty bag falling through to the SDK default. Run with:

```
git checkout fe6014339289bc039e368c1df94fe0729fd0b39d
bun run extensions/amazon-bedrock/bedrock-auth-config.realbehavior-driver.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**: Live terminal output, captured 2026-05-19 from the bun runtime (Node v24.3.0) running directly against the rebased branch tip. Not vitest, not snapshots, not lint — actual stdout from the helper resolving each scenario. Secrets redacted in the input bags before the run.


**Observed result after fix**: All five real legacy-shape inputs flow through `normalizeBedrockAuthConfig` and produce the documented `BedrockAuthConfig` shape with the right mode dispatch and the right defaults. The legacy `awsUseProfile: true` precedence is preserved (profile beats apikey when both are present, matching Cline's resolver). Invalid `reasoningEffort` values are silently dropped to `undefined`. The empty-bag baseline matches the docs accordion exactly. No exceptions thrown; bun process exit code 0.

**What was not tested**: End-to-end Bedrock signing/inference is intentionally out of scope here — that resolver lives in `@mariozechner/pi-ai` (https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/pull/3947) and is exercised once the pi-ai pin lands. This PR is the upstream config-surface owner only.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/amazon-bedrock` — 71 pass.

Broader fan-out (core/ui tests, package gates) deferred to CI/Testbox per repo policy.
- [ ] CI / Testbox: full Bedrock gauntlet.

target.title: fix(failover): classify 'budget exceeded' proxy errors as billing
target.body:
## Summary

Problem: OpenAI-compatible proxies return "Budget has been exceeded" on quota/budget overruns, but this message isn't in the billing pattern list, so it gets misclassified as a format error (on HTTP 400) or a generic rate limit (on HTTP 429).

Why it matters: Users see vague "something went wrong" or "rate limited, try again" messages and sit through unnecessary schema retries and model/provider fallbacks that can't possibly resolve a budget overrun, wasting time and burning through backup credentials.

What changed: Added a single case-insensitive "budget has been exceeded" pattern to ERROR_PATTERNS.billing in src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/failover-matches.ts, plus targeted test assertions covering isBillingErrorMessage and classifyFailoverReason for the new text on both HTTP 400 and429 paths.

What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to classification engine signatures, HTTP status routing, billing downstream handling, public API/config/CLI surface, other error categories (auth/rateLimit/overloaded/timeout), or any unrelated pattern — strictly one pattern added to an existing list.

target.title: fix: increase default gateway call timeout from 10000ms to 15000ms
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#74164 - Gateway timeout after 10000ms when sending large files via wecom channel.

The issue was that when no explicit `gateway.handshakeTimeoutMs` is configured and no `OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` env var is set, the `resolveGatewayCallTimeout` function would return `undefined` for `resolvedHandshakeTimeoutMs` and fall back to a hardcoded `10000ms`.

This is too short for large file uploads (e.g., 10MB files), causing the gateway call to timeout prematurely.

## Changes

- Modified `resolveGatewayCallTimeout` in `src/gateway/call.ts` to always call `resolvePreauthHandshakeTimeoutMs()` instead of returning `undefined` when no explicit config/env is set
- This ensures the proper default of `15000ms` (`DEFAULT_PREAUTH_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS`) is used instead of the hardcoded `10000ms`
- Updated the timeoutMs logic to use `resolvedHandshakeTimeoutMs` when either `hasEnvHandshakeTimeout` is true OR when `hasConfiguredHandshakeTimeout` is true AND `resolvedHandshakeTimeoutMs > 10000`

## Testing

Existing tests verify that:
- When `handshakeTimeoutMs: 30000` is set in config, the timeout is 30000ms
- When `OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS=30000` is set in env, the timeout is 30000ms
- These behaviors are preserved by the fix

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#74164

target.title: fix(sandbox): pass --init so tini reaps zombie processes
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem:
What: Adding --init to the docker sandbox makes tini the PID 1 and reaps zombie processes.
Why: Long-running sandboxes generate a large number of short-lived child processes (shell/browser helpers), and the entrypoint does not reap them → <defunct> processes accumulate → kernel.pid_max is exhausted → subsequent fork/exec fails.
Evidence: A new test was added to assert that --init appears in the output of buildSandboxCreateArgs and is placed after create; all 17 local tests passed.
Risk: Extremely low, --init is an official Docker feature, and the daemon comes with tini.

target.title: fix(browser): fall back to Hyprland grim capture for headed viewport screenshots
target.body:
Closes #54470

### Problem
On native Windows, `openclaw webhooks gmail setup --account` crashed immediately with
`Error: spawn gcloud ENOENT`. Node's `child_process.spawn` with `shell: false` does not
consult `PATHEXT`, so bare `gcloud`, `gog`, and `tailscale` are not found even when
`where.exe` resolves them to valid `.cmd` / `.exe` paths.

### Solution
Add `resolveExecutable(cmd)` in `src/infra/executable-path.ts`:
- No-op on non-Windows (single comparison, zero side-effects)
- No-op if `cmd` already carries a known extension (`.com`, `.exe`, `.bat`, `.cmd`)
- On win32: calls `where.exe` via `execFileSync`, prefers `.cmd` → `.exe` → first result,
  falls back to original `cmd` on throw
- No `shell: true` anywhere

Thread the resolved binary through every affected spawn site:
- `gcloudBin`: module-level `??=` in `runGcloudCommand` — one `where.exe` for the entire setup sequence
- `tailscaleBin`: local variable in `ensureTailscaleEndpoint` — one `where.exe` for both `runCommandWithTimeout` calls
- `gogBin`: module-level `??=` shared by `startGmailWatch` and `spawnGogServe` — one `where.exe` for the process lifetime, zero extra calls on watcher restarts

### Files changed
- `src/infra/executable-path.ts` — new file, `resolveExecutable`
- `src/hooks/gmail-setup-utils.ts` — `gcloudBin` + `tailscaleBin`
- `src/hooks/gmail-watcher.ts` — `gogBin`
- `src/infra/executable-path.test.ts` — 10 unit tests (all 5 resolution branches + `lines[0]` fallback)
- `src/hooks/gmail-setup-utils.test.ts` — 3 tests covering the new cached resolution

### Test results
pnpm test src/hooks/gmail-setup-utils.test.ts src/hooks/gmail.test.ts src/hooks/gmail-watcher-lifecycle.test.ts ✅
pnpm test src/process/exec.windows.test.ts src/process/windows-command.test.ts src/infra/executable-path.test.ts ✅
pnpm check:changed ✅

### Notes
- `src/process/exec.ts` is untouched — existing package-manager shim is unaffected
- Fix is fully inert on macOS and Linux
- Tested against reporter evidence: `where.exe gcloud` resolving to `C:\Tools\gcloud\gcloud.cmd`

target.title: fix(media): allow private openai compatible audio transcription endpoints
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Audio media-understanding transcription could target self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoints, but audio config did not expose the private-network request opt-in used by guarded provider HTTP.
- Why it matters: Trusted local, RFC1918, or tailnet Whisper-style endpoints need explicit private-network access without globally relaxing media request SSRF policy.
- What changed: Added audio-only request config support for `tools.media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork`, aligned schema/generated metadata/docs, threaded audio request overrides into provider execution, and aligned OpenAI-compatible loopback audio auth with the chat-completion local auth pattern by using the shared `custom-local` marker only for loopback OpenAI-compatible audio bases.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Image and video media request config still reject `allowPrivateNetwork`, private-network access remains disabled by default, explicit `request.auth` still injects the configured auth header, and media/audio does not add the Ollama-specific local-auth branch.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes N/A
- Related N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Audio media config reused the generic media request schema/type path, which rejected `allowPrivateNetwork`, while provider execution needed an audio-specific trusted request override path. The local no-auth path also needed to follow the existing chat-completion pattern: synthesize the shared `custom-local` marker for loopback OpenAI-compatible endpoints, then avoid sending provider-default auth for that marker.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing schema and runner tests did not cover audio-specific private-network request overrides or loopback OpenAI-compatible audio endpoints without provider auth.
- Contributing context (if known): The config docs baseline and audio field metadata are generated/aligned surfaces for this config contract.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/config/schema.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/runner.proxy.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/shared.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Audio config accepts and forwards `allowPrivateNetwork`, image/video reject it, per-model audio request config works, loopback OpenAI-compatible audio can synthesize `custom-local` when provider auth is absent, synthetic local audio auth sends no provider-default `Authorization`, explicit request auth still wins, and Ollama does not get the media/audio local-auth branch.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests exercise the config contract and provider request assembly without needing a live transcription service.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can opt audio transcription requests into private-network endpoints with `tools.media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork` and per-model audio request config. Loopback OpenAI-compatible audio endpoints such as `http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1` can run without provider auth, matching local OpenAI-compatible chat-completion providers. The OpenAI provider docs now show the loopback example base URL.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: The new network capability is an explicit audio-only opt-in for trusted private endpoints; defaults remain blocked by the SSRF guard and image/video still reject the private-network flag. Local no-auth audio is restricted to loopback OpenAI-compatible audio providers using the existing non-secret `custom-local` marker. Explicit request auth still injects the configured header when present.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.1.0-44-cloud-amd64 x86_64
- Runtime/container: Node v22.22.2, pnpm 10.33.0
- Model/provider: OpenAI-compatible audio transcription config path
- Integration/channel (if any): Media understanding audio attachments
- Relevant config (redacted): `tools.media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork`, `tools.media.audio.request.auth`, `tools.media.audio.models[].baseUrl`

### Steps

1. Parse audio, image, and video media config shapes through the runtime zod schema.
2. Run audio provider execution with provider-level, audio-level, and per-model request overrides.
3. Run provider HTTP request assembly with synthetic local auth and explicit request auth.

### Expected

- Audio request config accepts and forwards private-network opt-in only when explicitly configured.
- Image/video reject `allowPrivateNetwork` under media request config.
- Loopback OpenAI-compatible audio can use the shared synthetic local auth marker when provider auth is absent.
- Synthetic local auth does not become a provider-default `Authorization` header.
- Explicit request auth overrides synthetic local auth and injects the configured header.
- Ollama does not receive a media/audio local-auth special case.

### Actual

- Matches expected in local tests and changed-gate validation.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Local validation:

## Human Verification (required)

Set up a (WhisperLive)[https://github.com/collabora/WhisperLive] server locally. Modified openclaw.json to point to it with `media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork = true`:

```
"media": {
      "audio": {
        "enabled": true,
        "language": "en",
        "models": [
          {
            "provider": "openai",
            "model": "whisper-1",
            "baseUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1",
          }
        ],
        "request": {

        }

```

Verified audio transcription:

```
$ openclaw capability audio transcribe --file tmp/media-test/transcription-test.wav --language en --json
{
  "ok": true,
  "capability": "audio.transcribe",
  "transport": "local",
  "attempts": [],
  "outputs": [
    {
      "path": "/home/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/tmp/media-test/transcription-test.wav",
      "text": "Open Claw Media Transcription Test. The passphrase is Brass Lobster 7.",
      "kind": "audio.transcription"
    }

}
```



## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No PR review conversations currently exist.

- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes, optional new audio request config only
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Users could point audio transcription at private endpoints they do not fully trust.
  - Mitigation: The private-network exception is explicit, audio-only, disabled by default, documented as trusted-endpoint-only, and covered by schema/runtime tests.
- Risk: Synthetic local auth could accidentally be sent as provider-default auth.
  - Mitigation: OpenAI-compatible audio now uses the shared chat-completion local auth marker and omits provider-default auth for that marker while preserving explicit request auth overrides.

target.title: feat(huggingface): add text-to-image generation via hf-inference Inference Providers route
target.body:
## Summary

Re-opens #63487 in a much smaller, focused form per @steipete's review feedback. Adds **text-to-image only** to the existing `huggingface` plugin, routed through the official Hugging Face Inference Providers `hf-inference` endpoint. No new credentials, no parallel HTTP stack, no out-of-scope capabilities.

Closes / supersedes #63487.

## What's in scope

- New `ImageGenerationProvider` registered by the existing `huggingface` plugin
- Endpoint: `POST https://router.huggingface.co/hf-inference/models/<repo_id>` (returns raw image bytes)
- Default model: `black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-Krea-dev`; recommended list also exposes FLUX.1-dev / FLUX.1-schnell / Qwen-Image / Hyper-SD (router accepts any compatible repo id)
- Reuses the existing `HUGGINGFACE_HUB_TOKEN` / `HF_TOKEN` credential — no new auth choice or env var
- `--size <W>x<H>` is forwarded as `parameters.width` / `parameters.height` per the [text-to-image task spec](https://huggingface.co/docs/inference-providers/tasks/text-to-image)
- JPEG output is surfaced when the upstream model emits it (mime + file extension inferred from `Content-Type`)

## Addressing the previous review

All five blockers from #63487 are handled:

1. **`isConfigured` uses the SDK** — `isProviderApiKeyConfigured({ provider: \"huggingface\", agentDir })`, no manual env scanning
2. **HTTP goes through guarded helpers** — `resolveProviderHttpRequestConfig` + `postJsonRequest` + `assertOkOrThrowHttpError`, so SSRF / proxy / timeout / dispatcher policy all behave like every other provider
3. **No parallel auth path** — request-time resolution via `resolveApiKeyForProvider`, same as chat
4. **Plugin manifest declares the contract** — `contracts.imageGenerationProviders: [\"huggingface\"]` in `openclaw.plugin.json`
5. **Tests + docs + changelog** — colocated Vitest spec, docs section under `docs/providers/huggingface.md`, changelog entry under the Unreleased section with `Thanks @lyfuci`

## Out of scope (deliberate)

Per maintainer guidance, this PR is intentionally tight. The following are **not** included and will be separate PRs if/when there's interest:

- Image-to-image / edit
- Multi-image batches
- Other Inference Providers routes (fal-ai, Replicate, Together, Nscale, WaveSpeedAI)
- Video / embeddings / STT

## Test plan

- [x] \`pnpm check:changed\` — 6 lanes green (conflict markers, typecheck extensions, typecheck extension tests, lint extensions, runtime import cycles, tests changed)
- [x] 7 new Vitest cases pass (default model, custom model + size, JPEG mime inference, missing key, upstream HTTP error, empty payload, capability surface)
- [x] Format check clean on the 6 touched files
- [x] End-to-end smoke test against the real \`hf-inference\` endpoint:
  \`\`\`bash
  openclaw infer image generate \\
    --model huggingface/black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-schnell \\
    --prompt \"a serene lake surrounded by mountains at sunset, photorealistic\" \\
    --size 1024x1024 \\
    --output lake.png
  # → 1024x1024 PNG, 800 KB, file lake.png: PNG image data, 1024 x 1024, 8-bit/color RGB
  \`\`\`

target.title: feat(tasks): improve task state transitions and lifecycle formatting
target.body:
## Summary
- add explicit intermediate active task states for approval waits and external waits
- propagate those active-state semantics across registry maintenance, flow guards, CLI views, and auto-reply task rendering
- add compressed operational summaries plus shared task lifecycle event formatting for state-change updates
- reuse the shared lifecycle formatter in task update delivery paths and extend focused task coverage

## Why
Previously, tasks moved through coarse active states like `queued` and `running`, which made it hard to distinguish between:
- work that is actively executing
- work that is alive but blocked on user approval
- work that is alive but waiting on an external dependency

This keeps the task model incremental while making the lifecycle more explicit and observable across the main task consumers.

## Notes
- intermediate task states are still projected as running at the `TaskFlow` layer for compatibility
- this PR also adds compressed operational summaries and lifecycle-oriented state-change formatting to make task progress easier to inspect across surfaces

## Testing
- OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test:fast -- src/tasks/task-status.test.ts src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts
- OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test:fast -- src/tasks/task-status.test.ts src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts src/tasks/task-registry.store.test.ts

target.title: fix(slack): remove socket reconnect attempt cap so gateway stays connected indefinitely
target.body:
## What and why

After 12 consecutive failed Slack socket reconnects the provider threw an error and exited permanently. The gateway never restarts providers after they exit, so all subsequent Slack messages were silently dropped while the bot still appeared online in Slack (bots always show green in Slack's UI regardless of socket state).

This is the root cause of #72808. Users who hit a network blip or Slack server restart at the wrong time would lose Slack entirely until they manually restarted OpenClaw.

## Fix

Set `maxAttempts: 0` in `SLACK_SOCKET_RECONNECT_POLICY`. The existing `maxAttempts > 0 &&` guard in `provider.ts` already treats `0` as unlimited — this convention was clearly intentional given the `|| "∞"` fallback already present in the log format strings.

Non-recoverable auth errors (`account_inactive`, `invalid_auth`, `token_revoked`, etc.) already bail immediately via `isNonRecoverableSlackAuthError` before the `maxAttempts` check runs, so removing the cap does not create an infinite retry loop for permanent credential failures.

With `maxAttempts: 0` the reconnect log now reads `retry N/∞ in Xs`, which is the correct behavior for a long-running gateway service.

## Testing

- 813 Slack extension tests pass unchanged (no test asserted on `maxAttempts` behavior)
- Extension prod + test typecheck lanes green

Fixes #72808

target.title: Fix Android node system.run shell wrapper
target.body:
Title: Fix Android node system.run shell wrapper

Summary:
- Use Termux's shell path for Android node system.run wrappers.
- Keep Windows and other POSIX platforms unchanged.
- Document the Android/Termux node-host shell behavior.

Problem:
Android/Termux does not provide /bin/sh. When an Android node advertises system.run, exec host=node builds a POSIX wrapper with /bin/sh -lc, causing execution to fail with spawn /bin/sh ENOENT.

Change:
- Adds ANDROID_TERMUX_SHELL = /data/data/com.termux/files/usr/bin/sh.
- buildNodeShellCommand now returns that shell when node platform is android.
- Adds unit coverage for android and case/space normalization.
- Adds docs notes in docs/cli/node.md and docs/nodes/index.md.

Validation:
- git diff --check passed.
- Full vitest was not run locally because dependency installation was killed by the host before node_modules completed.

target.title: fix(tui): follow active gateway port
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw tui` could still build its default local Gateway URL from config/defaults after a Gateway was started with an explicit runtime `--port`.
- Why it matters: local TUI users with a custom active Gateway port had to pass `--url` manually or the client tried `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`.
- What changed: Gateway lock metadata now records the resolved runtime port, and TUI reads that live lock for local default connections before building the loopback URL.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): explicit `--url`, remote Gateway config, auth selection, and non-loopback websocket security precedence are preserved.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #42461
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the TUI connection resolver built its local loopback URL from config/defaults and had no process-local source for the already-running Gateway's CLI `--port` override.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression test asserted that a Gateway running on `48789` makes default `openclaw tui` connect to `ws://127.0.0.1:48789`.
- Contributing context (if known): the Gateway already used a per-config lock for active runtime ownership, but the lock payload did not include the resolved port.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/tui/gateway-chat.test.ts`, `src/infra/gateway-lock.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: active lock port `48789` drives default TUI URL resolution while explicit `--url` and remote config still win.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it tests the lock metadata seam and the TUI resolver directly without starting a real Gateway process.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw tui` without `--url` now follows the active local Gateway runtime port recorded by the running Gateway.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
openclaw gateway --port 48789 -> openclaw tui -> ws://127.0.0.1:18789

After:
openclaw gateway --port 48789 -> active lock records 48789 -> openclaw tui -> ws://127.0.0.1:48789
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local dev
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm via npm exec
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): TUI + local Gateway
- Relevant config (redacted): local Gateway mode

### Steps

1. Start the Gateway with a non-default port such as `openclaw gateway --port 48789`.
2. Run `openclaw tui` without `--url`.
3. Inspect the resolved Gateway connection target.

### Expected

- TUI connects to `ws://127.0.0.1:48789`.

### Actual

- Before this fix, TUI could connect to `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - `CI=true OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=0 npm exec pnpm@10.33.0 -- test src/tui/gateway-chat.test.ts src/infra/gateway-lock.test.ts`
  - `npm exec pnpm@10.33.0 -- exec oxfmt --check src/infra/gateway-lock.ts src/infra/gateway-lock.test.ts src/tui/gateway-chat.ts src/tui/gateway-chat.test.ts docs/cli/tui.md docs/gateway/troubleshooting.md`
  - `CI=true OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=0 npm exec pnpm@10.33.0 -- run check:changed`
- Edge cases checked: explicit URL override wins; remote Gateway config wins; stale/non-Gateway lock owner is ignored.
- What you did **not** verify: a manual live TUI session against a real Gateway process.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: stale lock metadata could point TUI at the wrong port.
  - Mitigation: the reader only trusts a lock whose owner still appears alive as a Gateway and whose recorded port is busy; explicit `--url` remains the override.

## Maintainer Verification - 2026-05-30

Behavior addressed: default `openclaw tui` Gateway resolution follows the active local Gateway lock port when a Gateway is running on a non-default port.

Real environment tested: macOS source checkout at `4f4b04875ad`, isolated OpenClaw home/state, Gateway auth mode `none`, loopback Gateway on `48789`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```shell
node --import tsx src/entry.ts gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --auth none --bind loopback --port 48789 --ws-log compact --verbose
node --import tsx --input-type=module   # imported src/tui/gateway-chat.ts, called resolveGatewayConnection({}), GatewayChatClient.connect({}), waitForReady(), getGatewayStatus()
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
process title: openclaw-gateway
resolved_url=ws://127.0.0.1:48789
connected_url=ws://127.0.0.1:48789
gateway_status=ok
[ws] connect client=openclaw-tui clientDisplayName=openclaw-tui version=2026.5.30 mode=ui platform=darwin auth=none localPort=48789
```

Observed result after fix: the TUI Gateway client resolved and connected to the custom active Gateway port `48789`, then successfully called `status`.

What was not tested: full human keystroke rendering inside the terminal UI; this proof covers the TUI Gateway connection/resolver path and real WebSocket handshake, not terminal paint behavior.

target.title: fix(webchat): persist chat queue to localStorage with drain-on-reconnect
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes #51549 — WebChat loses queued messages on browser refresh or session switch.

## Problem
chatQueue was stored as a pure in-memory array. On browser refresh or session switch, the array was recreated empty and all queued messages were permanently lost.

## Solution
Persists chatQueue to localStorage on every mutation, with automatic restore on reconnect or session switch.

## Changes

- **ui/src/ui/chat-queue-persistence.ts** (new): persistChatQueue + restoreChatQueue with 24h TTL
- **ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts**: call persistQueue after every chatQueue mutation (enqueue, steer, drain, remove)
- **ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts**: export flushChatQueue wrapper for lifecycle use
- **ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.ts**: restore queue from localStorage on reconnect + auto-drain
- **ui/src/ui/app-render.ts**: restore queue on session switch

## Notes
- Session-store rotation/recovery is handled in PR #71328
- Queue persistence keys are removed when queue drains to empty (avoids localStorage bloat)
- Restored items are de-duplicated by message id against current in-memory queue

Fixes #51549

target.title: fix(cron): add .catch() re-arm and watchdog to prevent runtime timer chain death
target.body:
## Summary

When the cron `setTimeout` chain breaks at runtime, the scheduler silently stops firing and never recovers until a gateway restart. This was observed after ~2.5 days of continuous gateway uptime on macOS (Apple Silicon) with no errors logged.

**Root cause**: The `.catch()` handler in `armTimer()` and `armRunningRecheckTimer()` logs the error but does NOT call `armTimer(state)`, so if `onTimer()` ever rejects without reaching its `finally` block, the timer chain is permanently broken.

**Contributing factor**: On macOS, background processes (like the gateway running as a launchd agent) are subject to App Nap and timer coalescing. While `MAX_TIMER_DELAY_MS = 60s` should be short enough for the clamped `setTimeout` to survive, the OS can still defer callbacks in edge cases, and if the chain breaks once, there is no recovery mechanism.

## Changes

### 1. Re-arm in `.catch()` handler (`timer.ts`)

Both `armTimer()` and `armRunningRecheckTimer()` now call `armTimer(state)` inside `.catch()`, ensuring the timer chain is never permanently broken:

```ts
void onTimer(state).catch((err) => {
  state.deps.log.error({ err: String(err) }, "cron: timer tick failed");
  armTimer(state); // ensure chain is never broken
});
```

### 2. Independent watchdog `setInterval` (`timer.ts`)

Added `startCronWatchdog()` — a `setInterval`-based watchdog (every 5 minutes) that detects when `nextWakeAtMs` is past-due by more than `MAX_TIMER_DELAY_MS` and force-triggers `onTimer` + re-arms the chain. `setInterval` is more resilient to OS-level timer deferral than chained `setTimeout` because libuv treats it as a persistent/repeating timer.

### 3. Lifecycle integration (`ops.ts`, `state.ts`)

- Watchdog is started in `start()` and stopped in `stop()`
- Added `_stopWatchdog` field to `CronServiceState`

### 4. Tests (`timer.watchdog.test.ts`)

- Watchdog detects stalled timer and logs warning
- Watchdog does not false-positive on healthy timers
- Watchdog respects `cronEnabled: false`
- Cleanup function stops the watchdog

## Relationship to other PRs

- **#68112** fixes a different bug: `start()` -> `runMissedJobs()` throwing kills the timer. That is a startup-time issue.
- **This PR** fixes a **runtime** issue: the timer chain dying during normal operation after hours/days of uptime.

The two fixes are complementary and non-overlapping.

Fixes #73166

target.title: fix(gateway): skip pricing bootstrap in replace mode
target.body:
## Problem

When `models.mode: "replace"` is configured, Gateway startup still bootstraps remote OpenRouter/LiteLLM pricing metadata by default. That surprises users who intentionally replaced the model catalog and can add avoidable network work during startup.

## Root cause

`models.pricing.enabled` previously defaulted to enabled independently of `models.mode`, so replace-mode configs still loaded the remote pricing bootstrap path unless users explicitly disabled pricing.

## Complete fix boundary

- Runtime default: `models.pricing.enabled` now remains an explicit override, but when omitted it defaults to disabled for `models.mode: "replace"` and enabled otherwise.
- Startup/cache behavior: bootstrap and refresh paths respect the derived pricing enablement.
- User/model-visible surfaces: schema help, generated base schema, config docs, model docs, token-use docs, config tools docs, generated config baseline, and changelog all describe the replace-mode default and explicit opt-in.
- Regression coverage: direct pricing cache behavior, Gateway startup import behavior, schema/help wording, and existing adjacent startup/cron shards.

## What intentionally did not change

- `models.pricing.enabled: true` still force-enables pricing bootstrap in replace mode.
- Non-replace modes keep the existing default pricing behavior.
- Pricing provider URL/cache/storage semantics are unchanged.
- Model resolution and provider routing semantics are unchanged.

## Tests run

Local on head `4aeef953afbc3cf57ef3d92f7d972703659ca092`:

- `pnpm config:schema:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm test src/gateway/model-pricing-cache.test.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-plugins.test.ts src/cron/service.read-ops-nonblocking.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/model-pricing-config.ts src/gateway/model-pricing-cache.test.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/config/schema.base.generated.ts`
- `git diff --check FETCH_HEAD...HEAD`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm docs:list`

## Linked issue

Fixes #74020.

## CI red analysis

Current head `4aeef953afbc3cf57ef3d92f7d972703659ca092` has a fresh CI run in progress after rebasing onto upstream fixes for agent wait/deferred dispatch behavior. GitHub currently reports the PR as mergeable; no conflict is shown.

Previous superseded head `4428c0c272665e76728b9fd38630dec457bf82df` had all regular CI checks passing. The only completed red check was the parity gate: OpenAI lane passed 12/12, Opus 4.6 lane passed 11/12 and failed only `instruction-followthrough-repo-contract` with `agent.wait returned timeout: no error`. The artifact/logs place that failure in qa-channel/agent followthrough behavior, not in this PR's pricing bootstrap, schema/help, docs, or changelog diff. I do not have repository admin rights to rerun failed checks directly, so the branch was rebased and pushed to pick up upstream qa/agent wait fixes and start a new run.

Earlier superseded reds after older heads were also outside this diff and were resolved by rebasing to upstream fixes: one gateway startup plugin mock missing `pruneUnknownBundledRuntimeDepsRoots`, and one cron startup catch-up timing issue.

target.title: [codex] Harden Slack delivery retries
target.body:
## Summary
- retry Slack chat.postMessage delivery on short-lived socket/write failures
- avoid treating queued replies as delivered when the dispatcher recorded delivery failures
- add focused coverage for transient send retry and failed delivery accounting

## Root Cause
Slack turn completion could infer user-facing delivery from queued reply counts even when delivery failed asynchronously. Slack write calls also returned immediately on transient socket readiness errors, which can happen during reconnect windows.

## Validation

No credentials, operational logs, or local runtime configuration are included.
